title: Telosma cordata
taxonomy: Telosma cordata is classified under the kingdom Plantae, within the family Apocynaceae, and belongs to the order Gentianales. This plant is a species of the genus Telosma, which is native to tropical regions. The classification of Telosma cordata further breaks down into the family Apocynaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The order Gentianales is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many families. The classification of Telosma cordata is a key part of understanding the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
uses: Telosma cordata, also known as the heartleaf or heart vine, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Telosma cordata, also known as the Honeysuckle vine, is a climbing plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and heart-shaped leaves.
